channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: TikTok is obsessed with this investor who bought 30 floors of a Chicago skyscraper
item.description: One of the more unique takes on the POV trend on TikTok: “POV: You bought a 100-year-old skyscraper . . .
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Instagram launches ‘Blend’ to share personalized Reels with friends
item.description: When it comes to sharing Instagram Reels with friends, the process of three taps to get a Reel from A to B can feel surprisingly tedious. Now, Instagram has addressed that issue with its latest feature: Instagram Blend.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Nvidia watches its Trump overtures come to naught
item.description: Welcome to AI Decoded, Fast Company’s weekly newsletter that breaks down the most important news in the world of AI. You can sign up to receive this newsletter every week here.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Netflix's first quarter builds on recent momentum as trade war drags down other tech companies
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Netflix fared better than analysts anticipated during the first three months of the year, signaling the world’s largest video streaming service is still thriving as President Donald Trump’s policies cast a pall on the economy.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: San Jose mayor Mahan, local congressman Liccardo lend support as Waymo applies to bring robotaxis to the city
item.description: Waymo initially faced a rocky road in bringing its pioneering technology to Bay Area streets and highways.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Baron, The Mercury News

item.title: What Strava buying Runna means for users of both fitness apps – according to their CEOs
item.description: "We're keeping the two apps separate for the foreseeable": Strava and Runna CEOs tell us their acquisition won't be like Fitbit and Google
item.dc:creator: matt.evans@futurenet.com (Matt Evans), TechRadar

item.title: Google's digital ad network declared an illegal monopoly, joining its search engine in penalty box
item.description: Google has been branded an abusive monopolist by a federal judge for the second time in less than a year, this time for illegally exploiting some of its online marketing technology to boost the profits fueling an internet empire currently worth $1.8 trillion.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Cuba's Restaurant Revolution: A Glimpse into the Future - Havana's Doña Alicia
item.description: HAVANA, Cuba — Amidst the island's technological scarcity, Havana's Doña Alicia restaurant has emerged as a beacon of innovation, embracing digital menus, Alexa-enabled assistance, and even a food-delivering robot.
item.dc:creator: ANDREA RODRÍGUEZ, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Smartphones may boost children's mental health, study finds
item.description: The study's findings challenge the assumption that kids who own smartphones tend to fare worse than those who don't.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Shafer, NewsNation

item.title: I’ve never seen so many bug fixes in an Nvidia GPU driver, as new release offers vital cures for black screen crashes, Windows 11 24H2 glitches and more
item.description: Nvidia only talks about fixing black screen woes for RTX 5000 graphics cards, so what about past-gen GPUs? Fortunately, it seems like good news here, too.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: CIOs must mind their own data confidence gap
item.description: As far as many C-suite business and IT executives are concerned, their company data is in great shape, capable of fueling data-driven decision-making and delivering AI-powered solutions.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Trump's potential semiconductor tariffs spook tech industry
item.description: President Trump’s potential tariffs on semiconductors are stoking alarm within the technology sector as companies brace for ripple effects across the industry and its competitive standing on a global stage.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: New iPhone 17 Pro render ditches the two-tone design for a possibly more accurate appearance
item.description: A new iPhone 17 Pro render differs from earlier ones as it doesn't show a two-tone design, but this might be more accurate.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: More Oblivion Remastered details, including a release date, have seemingly been outed by Xbox's own support account
item.description: The Oblivion Remastered release date and deluxe edition details have seemingly been confirmed by Xbox Support of all places.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg wraps up testimony in antitrust case
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg emphasized in federal court on Wednesday that he bought Instagram and WhatsApp because he saw value in the companies — not to take out competitors, as the Federal Trade Commission alleges in a historic antitrust trial.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: The cybersecurity law that quietly underpins U.S. digital defenses is about to expire
item.description: Nearly a decade after Congress passed the Cybersecurity Information Sharing Act of 2015, the law is facing an uncertain future.
item.dc:creator: Max Ufberg, Fast Company

item.title: Hackers steal 1.6 million patient records in major healthcare breach
item.description: The CyberGuy explains why healthcare organizations are vulnerable to hackers after there have been a half dozen data breaches this year.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A handy new Android memory superpower
item.description: Tell me if this sounds familiar: You stumble onto something scintillating during your various digital dalliances — something you think might be a useful nugget of knowledge for work or maybe even a pertinent update for your personal life.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: 6 languages you can deploy to WebAssembly right now
item.description: WebAssembly, or Wasm, gives developers a way to create programs that run at near-native speed in the browser or anywhere else you can deploy the WebAssembly runtime. But you generally don’t write programs in Wasm directly.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: The programming language wars
item.description: Microsoft recently decided to rewrite the TypeScript tool chain using Go. This has caused a stir as folks wonder why Microsoft chose the Go language instead of their own C# or even TypeScript itself. I have to say that it is a curious choice.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Don't like a columnist's opinion? Los Angeles Times offers an AI-generated opposing viewpoint
item.description: In a colorful commentary for the Los Angeles Times, Matt K. Lewis argued that callousness is a central feature of the second Trump administration, particularly its policies of deportation and bureaucratic cutbacks.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Meta CEO Zuckerberg considered spinning off Instagram in 2018 over antitrust worries, email says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg once considered separating Instagram from its parent company due to worries about antitrust litigation, according to an email shown Tuesday on the second day of an antitrust trial alleging Meta illegally monopolized the social media market.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: JRuby 10 brings faster startup times
item.description: JRuby 10, the latest release of the Ruby language variant built atop the JVM, has arrived, bringing startup time improvements, support for Java 21, and compatibility with Ruby 3.4. Release of JRuby 10 was announced April 14. JRuby 10 can be downloaded from jruby.org.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: New Models from OpenAI: GPT-4.1 Family Outperforms Predecessors
item.description: OpenAI announces new models, GPT-4.1 family, which outperform predecessors in various functions.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Cato Networks augments CASB with genAI security
item.description: Cato Networks recently unveiled new generative AI capabilities in its Cloud Access Security Broker (CASB) that the secure access service edge (SASE) provider says will let enterprise IT organizations detect, analyze, and gain insights into the use of genAI applications.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: China pursuing 3 alleged US operatives over cyberattacks during Asian Games
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — China said Tuesday it is pursuing three alleged U.S. operatives accused of carrying out cyberattacks on Chinese infrastructure during the Asian Games held in the city of Harbin in February. A notice from the Harbin police headquarters named them as Katheryn A. Wilson, Robert J.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER BODEEN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Accelerate Together
item.description: CrowdStrike’s field tech strategist explains how AWS Control Tower plus CrowdStrike Falcon lets new workloads inherit access controls in zero trust environments The post Accelerate Together — Zero Trust 2025: CrowdStrike’s Dennen Monks on ensuring none of your assets escape zero trust coverage first
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Windows Server 2025 domain controllers may lose connectivity after reboot, says Microsoft
item.description: Microsoft is warning IT administrators that their Windows Server 2025 domain controllers (DC) may lose network connectivity after they are restarted.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Nvidia plans to manufacture AI chips in the US for the first time
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Nvidia announced Monday that it will produce its artificial intelligence supercomputers in the United States for the first time.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: More than $35,000 recovered in stolen Bitcoin scam for Ohio woman
item.description: An Ohio woman who fell victim to a cryptocurrency scam has been refunded most of the nearly $42,000 in stolen Bitcoin.
item.dc:creator: Adam Conn, NewsNation

item.title: Meta says it will resume AI training with public content from European users
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Social media company Meta said Monday that it will start using publicly available content from European users to train its artificial intelligence models, resuming work put on hold last year after activists raised concerns about data privacy.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: As legacy of Ford fades in Brazil, China's BYD looks to extend global lead in EV sales
item.description: CAMACARI, Brazil (AP) — When Chinese automaker BYD Co. announced plans to build a massive factory in the hardscrabble city of Camacari, in Brazil’s northeastern Bahia state, locals saw a new beginning. After years of economic stagnation following Ford Motor Co.
item.dc:creator: FABIANO MAISONNAVE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Teen goes from 10 nightly seizures to zero with brain implant
item.description: The NeuroOne OneRF Ablation System is a new device with FDA-cleared technology designed for both diagnosing and treating neurological disorders in one procedure.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: More and faster: New proposals changing Python from within
item.description: Top picks for Python readers on InfoWorld Making Python faster won’t be easy, but it’ll be worth it Python’s malleability and dynamism make it powerful, but also very hard to speed up. Here’s a look at some of the ways developers are working to make Python faster from within.
item.dc:creator: Info World
